title: Information for immigration advisers
body: "Overview  The Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) supports individuals seeking to become immigration advisers and those already regulated by the IAA. We provide the resources, guidance, and regulations necessary to ensure advisers deliver high-quality, compliant immigration advice.  This page provides key information about registration, assessments, and maintaining professional standards. Whether you’re just starting your journey or looking to advance your career as an immigration adviser, you’ll find the necessary guidance and resources you need.  Becoming an immigration adviser  This page provides all the information you need to become an immigration adviser, including details about the application and assessment process.   How to become a regulated immigration adviser   Applying for registration or to increase adviser level  This guidance outlines the process for becoming a registered adviser, including details on the application process, applying for the correct level and sitting competence assessments.   How to apply for registration with the IAA   Preparing for your competence assessments  This page contains all the essential information about competence assessments, including syllabuses, example questions, marking criteria, and assessment dates.   Online competence assessment process   Regulations that immigration advisers must follow  This page outlines the regulations that immigration advisers must follow, including the Code of standards and Guidance on competence.   Regulations that immigration advisers must follow   Resources for regulated organisations  This page provides policies, practice notes, and guidance to help regulated organisations meet IAA standards and deliver compliant immigration advice.   Resources for Regulated Organisations  "
